title: ARGS: Alignment as Reward-Guided Search
problem: Language model alignment: ensuring models' behaviors agree with human objectives and safety considerations

existing_approaches.name: Reinforcement Learning with Human Feedback (RLHF)
existing_approaches.description: A prominent alignment strategy that has gained widespread adoption among state-of-the-art models

existing_approaches.name: Proximal Policy Optimization (PPO)
existing_approaches.description: A pivotal component within RLHF that employs an external reward model
challenges: Need to repeat PPO training when altering the reward model hinders rapid customization

proposed_solution.name: Alignment as Reward-Guided Search (ARGS)
proposed_solution.description: A novel framework designed to enhance the alignment of generated text with human-desired preferences
proposed_solution.key_mechanism: At each decoding step, adjusts the model's probabilistic prediction using a reward signal
benefits: Maintains semantic relevance with respect to the previous context
Aligns with reward criteria and human preference
Flexible trading off between sub-goals with proper weighting
Can be integrated with various token selection strategies including greedy and stochastic sampling
Enables quick realignments without retraining the foundational model

results.qualitative_assessment: Less redundant and more informative outputs than standard maximum-likelihood decoding

contributions: Proposed a novel framework ARGS, which postulates the alignment process as a reward-guided search problem that runs during decoding
